gaps: Official X tooling does not provide a unified agent-facing CLI for the full v2 API plus browser-backed Articles authoring.
patterns: Read commands expose JSON-first output and dry-run request previews for agent workflows.

novel_features.name: Markdown to tweet thread
novel_features.description: Split a markdown file into a numbered tweet thread. Atom-aware splitter honors paragraphs, list items, and code fences. Numbering suffix is reserved before packing so final tweet length stays within 280 chars.

novel_features.name: Markdown to X Article
novel_features.description: Parse a markdown file with YAML frontmatter and convert the body to Draft.js content_state JSON for X Articles authoring.

novel_features_built.name: Markdown to tweet thread
novel_features_built.description: Split a markdown file into a numbered tweet thread. Atom-aware splitter honors paragraphs, list items, and code fences. Numbering suffix is reserved before packing so final tweet length stays within 280 chars.

novel_features_built.name: Markdown to X Article
novel_features_built.description: Parse a markdown file with YAML frontmatter and convert the body to Draft.js content_state JSON for X Articles authoring.

narrative.headline: X v2 API coverage with browser-backed Articles authoring.
narrative.value_prop: Use this CLI for JSON-first access to X resources and dry-run request construction. It includes curated commands for long-form Articles workflows that are not exposed as standard v2 endpoints.

quickstart.comment: Preview the Articles list request without sending it.

recipes.title: Preview recent post search
recipes.explanation: Build the recent search request in dry-run mode before using live credentials.

narrative.when_to_use: Use this CLI when an agent needs to inspect or automate X v2 resources, compose tweet threads, or prepare X Articles payloads with reproducible JSON output.
